content.text:
This is a smart move — generalize the narrow endpoint into a proper, reusable **read-only access layer for AI agents** so you never again hand out a DB password or build one-off endpoints. Let me design it and hand you a developer-ready brief.

The core idea: a **read-only MCP server** (the standard way AI agents connect to a system) sitting on a **read-only database role against a replica**, with hard guardrails — so any AI session gets safe, authenticated, audited, *read-only* access to the business data, and *structurally cannot* write, see secrets, or run away with an expensive query. The licensing `/lic/status-export` case becomes just one tool inside it.

Let me write the brief to a file you can hand over, then show it here:

input.content:
# Developer Brief — Safe Read-Only AI Access Layer for DesignFlow

**Audience:** the DesignFlow PLM backend/infra developer (has GCP + Cloud SQL access).
**Author intent:** Build a single, reusable, **authenticated read-only** access layer so AI agents
(Claude Code sessions, future automations) can query DesignFlow data **across all modules** without raw
DB credentials, without any write capability, and without exposing secrets or sensitive fields. This
generalizes the one-off `/lic/status-export` idea into a durable platform capability.

---

## 1. Objective

Stand up a **read-only MCP server** ("`designflow-readonly`") that exposes curated, safe, audited,
read-only access to the DesignFlow Postgres for AI agents. It must be:

- **Structurally read-only** — physically incapable of writes/DDL, enforced at the database role level,
  not just in code.
- **Credential-free for consumers** — agents authenticate with a scoped, revocable token; the real DB
  connection string never leaves the server.
- **Safe by default** — secrets, auth tables, and sensitive business fields (pricing/cost/margin, PII)
  are excluded or redacted unless explicitly scoped in.
- **Auditable & bounded** — every query logged; row caps, statement timeouts, and rate limits enforced.
- **Reusable** — one layer serves every AI session and every module (items, licensing, prod/sample
  tracking, RFQ, vendors, customers, merch groups), not a new endpoint per use-case.

(MCP = Model Context Protocol, the standard interface Claude/AI agents use to call external tools. If MCP
is undesirable, the same design can be exposed as a read-only REST gateway — see §9 — but MCP is preferred
because the consumers are AI agents.)

## 2. System context (what you already run)

- DesignFlow runs on **GCP** (project `lithe-breaker-323913`, region `us-central1`), **Cloud Run**
  services behind the BFF (Microsoft SSO / OIDC), backed by a **Cloud SQL Postgres** instance, schema
  `designflow`. Deploys happen via **Cloud Build triggers on git push**; secrets live in **Secret
  Manager**; services reach Cloud SQL through a **VPC connector**.
- Data domains (non-exhaustive): `itemHeader`/`itemDetail` (products/SKUs), `licensingStatus` +
  `LicenseFeedBacks` + `licensingMilestone` (licensor approval), `ProdOrderHeader`/`ProdOrderDetail`
  (production), sample-tracking tables, `RFQGroup`/`RFQItem`/`RFQVendor` (costing/quotes), `externalVendor`,
  customers, `merchGroup` (licensor/property/artist codes), `GridLayout`, association tables.
- Source-of-truth note: product master is synced in from the Coldlion ERP by `designflow-data-syncing`.

## 3. Recommended architecture

```
   AI agent (Claude Code, etc.)
        │  MCP over HTTPS, bearer token (scoped, revocable)
        ▼
   designflow-readonly  (new Cloud Run service)
        │  • enforces SELECT-only, row caps, timeouts, deny-lists, redaction, audit
        │  connects as role  df_ro  (SELECT-only)
        ▼
   Cloud SQL  →  READ REPLICA of the designflow DB   (preferred; falls back to read-only role on primary)
```

Three independent layers of "read-only," so no single failure grants writes:
1. **Database role** `df_ro`: `USAGE` on schema + `SELECT` on allowed tables only; no INSERT/UPDATE/
   DELETE/DDL; `ALTER ROLE df_ro SET default_transaction_read_only = on`. Point it at a **read replica**
   so AI traffic cannot load or lock the primary.
2. **Server enforcement**: reject anything that isn't a single read-only `SELECT`/`WITH` (no `;`-chained
   statements, no write keywords, no `COPY`, no `pg_read_file`, etc.); inject a hard `LIMIT`; set
   `statement_timeout`.
3. **Policy**: table allow/deny lists + column redaction applied before results are returned.

## 4. Hard requirements (the guardrails)

**Database**
- Dedicated login role `df_ro`, owned by nobody, granted only `USAGE` + `SELECT`. Explicitly **no**
  membership in owner/superuser roles. Verify with a write attempt that it fails.
- `statement_timeout = 15s`, `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout = 30s` on the role.
- Connect to a **read replica** if one exists / can be added; otherwise the read-only role on the primary
  with conservative connection-pool limits.

**Query enforcement (server side)**
- Accept only a **single** statement that is `SELECT` or `WITH … SELECT`. Reject multiple statements,
  comments hiding writes, and any DDL/DML keyword. Parse, don't regex-only (use a real SQL parser).
- Enforce a maximum row count (e.g. `LIMIT 5000`, with pagination) and a max result payload size.
- Block direct access to system catalogs except via the curated `describe_schema` tool.

**Data policy (allow/deny)**
- **Deny by default:** auth/identity tables (users, roles, sessions, password hashes), token/secret
  tables, and anything holding credentials. These must be unreachable, not just hidden.
- **Sensitive business fields** — pricing/cost/margin/royalty (RFQ + licensing costing columns) and PII
  (emails, phones, addresses) — **redacted or excluded by default**, exposed only under an explicit
  `pricing` / `pii` scope on the token. (The business explicitly requires pricing be hidden from most
  roles — honor that here.)
- A small, version-controlled **policy file** (allow tables, deny tables, redact columns, scopes) drives
  this, so the surface is reviewable in git.

**Auth & tokens**
- Per-consumer **bearer tokens** stored in Secret Manager, each with: an id, scopes (e.g. `read:items`,
  `read:licensing`, `read:vendors`, `pricing`, `pii`), an expiry, and a revocation switch.
- No token → no access. Rotating/revoking a token is a config change, not a redeploy.
- Optional: tie to the existing OIDC so a human's identity can be attached to a session.

**Audit & limits**
- Log **every** request: token id, tool, the SQL/filters, row count, duration, timestamp — to an audit
  table (write via a separate minimal-privilege path) or Cloud Logging.
- Per-token **rate limiting** and concurrency caps; reject queries projected to exceed cost/row/time caps.

**Never**
- No write tools, ever. No DDL. No returning secrets, env vars, connection strings, or credential
  columns. No pointing the consumer at the primary if a replica is feasible. No superuser/owner role.

## 5. MCP tool surface (what agents call)

Curated, structured, read-only tools — each returns JSON and names its source table:

- `describe_schema(collection?)` — safe, curated schema map: tables, columns, types, and which columns are
  redacted/scoped. (No raw `information_schema` dump.)
- Domain readers (filtered + paginated): `get_items`, `get_item_detail(item_num_id)`,
  `get_licensing_status(item)`, `get_feedback_catalog`, `get_prod_orders`, `get_sample_tracking`,
  `get_rfq`, `search_vendors`, `get_customers`, `get_merch_groups`.
- `run_select(sql, params, limit)` — the guarded escape hatch for ad-hoc reads: SELECT-only, runs as
  `df_ro`, enforces timeout + row cap + deny-list + redaction. This is what makes the layer cover
  "everything applicable" without a new endpoint per question.

The existing narrow need (`/lic/status-export` for the poppim sync) becomes simply the `get_licensing_status`
tool / a saved query against this layer — one consumer among many.

## 6. Deployment & ops (fits your existing model)

- New repo or subfolder → its own **Cloud Build trigger** so it deploys on push like everything else.
- New **Cloud Run service** `designflow-readonly`, with a **least-privilege service account**, **VPC
  connector** to reach Cloud SQL, and the `df_ro` connection string + tokens in **Secret Manager** (create
  the secrets in the same change that wires `--set-secrets`).
- Health endpoint; structured logging; min-instances tuned for latency vs cost.

## 7. Security / threat model (build defensively)

AI agents can be steered by prompt-injection in the data they read, so assume a consumer may be coaxed into
trying to over-read. Mitigations already above: SELECT-only role, deny-listed secret/auth tables, default
field redaction, row/time caps, audit, scoped+revocable tokens. **Additionally:** consider a human-in-the-
loop or extra scope for bulk exports (e.g. queries returning > N rows or touching PII), and alert on
anomalous volume per token.

## 8. Acceptance tests (definition of done)

1. `df_ro` cannot INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/CREATE/DROP — each fails with a permission error.
2. `run_select` rejects multi-statement, DDL, write keywords, `COPY`, and file/network functions.
3. Deny-listed tables (users/auth/secrets) are unreachable through every tool.
4. Pricing/PII columns are absent unless the token carries the `pricing`/`pii` scope.
5. Row cap + statement timeout are enforced (a deliberately huge/slow query is bounded, not fatal).
6. Every call appears in the audit log; revoking a token immediately denies access.
7. A real agent query (e.g. "licensing status + Brand Assurance for item MEZH1DYPN02") returns correct data.

## 9. Phasing & open decisions

**Phase 1 (covers "everything" safely, minimum build):** `df_ro` role + read replica + MCP server with
`run_select` + deny-lists + redaction + audit + one token.
**Phase 2:** curated domain tools, scopes, per-consumer tokens, schema docs.
**Phase 3:** OIDC identity binding, anomaly alerting, optional read-only REST facade for non-AI automation.

**Decisions to confirm:**
- Read **replica** vs read-only role on the **primary** (replica strongly preferred).
- **MCP** (recommended for AI consumers) vs/and a **REST** read gateway.
- Exact **deny tables** and **redact columns** lists (needs a quick pass with whoever owns pricing/PII
  policy — pricing/cost/margin/royalty and personal contact fields at minimum).
- Token issuance/rotation process and where the audit log lives.

---

### TL;DR for the developer
Create a SELECT-only Postgres role `df_ro` on a read replica; build a small Cloud Run **MCP server** that
connects as that role and exposes read-only tools (curated domain readers + a guarded `run_select`); gate
it with scoped, revocable bearer tokens in Secret Manager; deny secret/auth tables and redact pricing/PII
by default; cap rows + time; audit everything; deploy via a Cloud Build trigger. Result: any AI session
gets safe, read-only, audited access to all of DesignFlow with zero shared DB credentials and zero write risk.

generalizes the one-off `/lic/status-export` idea into a durable platform capability.

---

## 1. Objective

  not just in code.
- **Credential-free for consumers** — agents authenticate with a scoped, revocable token; the real DB
  connection string never leaves the server.
- **Safe by default** — secrets, auth tables, and sensitive business fields (pricing/cost/margin, PII)
  are excluded or redacted unless explicitly scoped in.
- **Auditable & bounded** — every query logged; row caps, statement timeouts, and rate limits enforced.
- **Reusable** — one layer serves every AI session and every module (items, licensing, prod/sample
  tracking, RFQ, vendors, customers, merch groups), not a new endpoint per use-case.

(MCP = Model Context Protocol, the standard interface Claude/AI agents use to call external tools. If MCP
is undesirable, the same design can be exposed as a read-only REST gateway — see §9 — but MCP is preferred
because the consumers are AI agents.)

## 2. System context (what you already run)

- DesignFlow runs on **GCP** (project `lithe-breaker-323913`, region `us-central1`), **Cloud Run**
  services behind the BFF (Microsoft SSO / OIDC), backed by a **Cloud SQL Postgres** instance, schema
  `designflow`. Deploys happen via **Cloud Build triggers on git push**; secrets live in **Secret
  Manager**; services reach Cloud SQL through a **VPC connector**.
- Data domains (non-exhaustive): `itemHeader`/`itemDetail` (products/SKUs), `licensingStatus` +
  `LicenseFeedBacks` + `licensingMilestone` (licensor approval), `ProdOrderHeader`/`ProdOrderDetail`
  (production), sample-tracking tables, `RFQGroup`/`RFQItem`/`RFQVendor` (costing/quotes), `externalVendor`,
  customers, `merchGroup` (licensor/property/artist codes), `GridLayout`, association tables.
- Source-of-truth note: product master is synced in from the Coldlion ERP by `designflow-data-syncing`.

## 3. Recommended architecture

content.text:
Done — the developer-ready brief is at [designflow-ai-readonly-access-brief.md](designflow-ai-readonly-access-brief.md). You can hand the whole file (or paste its contents) straight to your developer; it's written to them and self-contained.

## What it specifies
A **read-only MCP server** for DesignFlow with safety enforced at **three independent layers**, so no single bug or trick grants a write:
1. **Database** — a `SELECT`-only Postgres role (`df_ro`) on a **read replica**, physically unable to write.
2. **Server** — rejects anything that isn't a single read-only `SELECT`; injects row caps + query timeouts.
3. **Policy** — secret/auth tables are unreachable; pricing/cost/margin and personal data are redacted by default unless a token explicitly carries that scope.

Plus: scoped, revocable bearer tokens (no shared DB password ever), full audit logging, rate limits, and a guarded `run_select` "escape hatch" so it covers *any* read across *all* modules — not a new endpoint per question. It deploys through your existing push→Cloud Build→Cloud Run flow.

## How it ties back to the licensing work
Your narrow `/lic/status-export` need just becomes **one tool** (`get_licensing_status`) inside this layer — so building this *also* unblocks the poppim sync's permanent read access, the right way.

## Decisions your developer (with you) will need to settle
The brief lists them, but the ones that need *your* business input:
- **Which fields count as sensitive** — pricing/cost/margin/royalty and personal contact info at minimum (the docs are clear pricing must stay hidden from most roles). Someone has to bless the redaction list.
- **Read replica vs. read-only on the primary** (replica strongly preferred — keeps AI traffic off production).
- **MCP vs. also a REST gateway** (MCP for AI agents; REST optional for other automation).

## One honest caveat
Giving AI broad read access to a live business system is powerful and carries real risk (e.g. prompt-injection coaxing an agent into over-reading). The brief builds against that — default-deny on secrets/PII, row caps, audit, and a note to require human-in-the-loop or an extra scope for bulk/PII exports. Worth your developer treating that section as load-bearing, not optional.

If you'd like, I can go a step further than the brief and **scaffold the MCP server itself** (I have a skill for building MCP servers) — a working read-only server with the `df_ro` role SQL, the guardrails, and the tool stubs — so your developer reviews and deploys rather than builds from scratch. Want me to do that?

content.content:
===== README.md =====
# Designflow MCP

TypeScript MCP server for Designflow company context, API docs, current data, and image/attachment metadata.

This service intentionally uses a lazy-loaded registry. It does **not** register every Designflow operation as an MCP tool.

Start with [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) before changing code. It is the canonical operating guide and documentation router.

## Quick Start

```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env
npm run dev:http
```

The local HTTP server listens on `http://localhost:3010` by default:

```text
GET  /health
POST /mcp
```

## Transport

| Mode | Command | Use |
|---|---|---|
| Streamable HTTP | `npm run dev:http` | Shared/remote service, future Cloud Run deployment |
| stdio | `npm run dev:stdio` | Local AI clients that spawn MCP servers as child processes |

## Always-On Tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `health` | Server status and enabled hidden capability count |
| `list_capabilities` | Browse hidden operations by group/safety |
| `get_capability_details` | Inspect one operation's exact contract |
| `tool_search` | Search hidden operations by intent |
| `invoke_tool` | Execute one hidden operation by exact name |

All real Designflow operations live in `src/registry/tool-defs.ts` and are called through `invoke_tool`.

## Tool Flow

Recommended client flow:

```text
tool_search({ query: "rfq price code" })
get_capability_details({ name: "find_rfq_item_by_price_code" })
invoke_tool({ name: "find_rfq_item_by_price_code", args: { priceCode: "ABC123" } })
```

## Configuration

Docs-only tools work without credentials. API-backed tools need `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT`. Data-sync tools also need `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY`.

See [docs/configuration.md](docs/configuration.md) for the full variable list.

## Documentation

| Doc | Contents |
|---|---|
| [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) | Canonical developer/AI operating guide |
| [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) | System design and lazy registry architecture |
| [docs/development.md](docs/development.md) | Local setup, build, smoke-test, and debug workflow |
| [docs/configuration.md](docs/configuration.md) | Environment variables and tool enablement |
| [docs/deployment.md](docs/deployment.md) | Current deployment status and Docker path |

This first version is read-only. Write/destructive capabilities are deliberately not enabled.

===== package.json =====
{
  "name": "designflow-mcp",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "description": "Lazy-loaded MCP server for Designflow company context, API data, and images.",
  "scripts": {
    "dev:http": "tsx src/index.ts --http",
    "dev:stdio": "tsx src/index.ts --stdio",
    "build": "tsc -p tsconfig.json",
    "start": "node dist/index.js --http",
    "start:stdio": "node dist/index.js --stdio",
    "typecheck": "tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit",
    "test": "npm run build && vitest run"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.29.0",
    "express": "^4.22.1",
    "zod": "^4.2.1"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/express": "^4.17.25",
    "@types/node": "^22.19.3",
    "tsx": "^4.21.0",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3",
    "vitest": "^4.1.8"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20"
  }
}

===== tools-config.json =====
{
  "enabled_read_tools": [
    "get_company_context",
    "get_api_inventory",
    "get_backend_api_reference",
    "get_item_master_api_reference",
    "get_tracking_api_reference",
    "get_data_sync_api_reference",
    "get_frontend_api_consumption",
    "get_bff_api_reference",
    "get_current_user",
    "get_grid_access_level",
    "search_factories",
    "list_factories",
    "get_factory_country_map",
    "find_rfq_item_by_price_code",
    "get_rfq_item_by_id",
    "get_rfq_item_history",
    "get_item_images",
    "get_item_attachments",
    "get_item_library_by_number",
    "get_items_by_design_ref",
    "get_item_detail",
    "search_samples",
    "get_sample",
    "get_sample_events",
    "get_sample_photos",
    "get_license_images"
  ],
  "enabled_write_tools": []
}


===== .env.example =====
# Transport
MCP_PORT=3010

# Use the BFF bases where possible. These defaults match local frontend envs.
DESIGNFLOW_CORE_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5004/api/core
DESIGNFLOW_ITEM_MASTER_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5004/api/item_master
DESIGNFLOW_TRACKING_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5004/api/tracking
DESIGNFLOW_DATA_SYNC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5004/api/data_sync

# Optional user-scoped auth for tools that call Designflow APIs.
# Use a normal Designflow JWT. Do not commit real tokens.
DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT=

# Optional system-to-system key for data_sync calls.
DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY=

# Comma-separated tool names to add/remove without code changes.
# Example: DESIGNFLOW_MCP_DISABLED_TOOLS=get_data_sync_item_headers
DESIGNFLOW_MCP_ENABLED_TOOLS=
DESIGNFLOW_MCP_DISABLED_TOOLS=


===== src/ tree =====
src/clients/designflow-client.ts
src/config/settings.ts
src/index.ts
src/registry/catalog.ts
src/registry/doc-reader.ts
src/registry/groups.ts
src/registry/tool-defs.ts
src/server.ts
src/types.ts

===== docs/ =====
architecture.md
configuration.md
deployment.md
development.md

===== deploy artifacts? =====

content.text:
Cloned to `D:\repos\designflow-mcp`. I read through it — here are your two answers, straight.

## Does it have what we need? — **Partly. Good bones, but not the specific piece, and not "everything."**

**What it is:** an early (v0.1.0) **read-only** MCP server that reaches DesignFlow data by calling your **existing BFF APIs with a DesignFlow user JWT** — same lazy-registry pattern as your other MCP servers. Write tools are deliberately empty.

✅ **Right shape & philosophy** — read-only, curated tools, no write capability, and it already follows your `sandbox-albert`→`develop` branch flow.
✅ **Covers** — factories, RFQ items/history, item library/detail, samples (+ events/photos), item images/attachments, **license *images***, and API-reference docs.
❌ **Missing the exact thing the poppim sync needs** — there is **no licensing-*status* tool.** Nothing reads `/lic/getLic` / the `licensingStatus` events, the **Brand Assurance #**, the sample number, or the `LicenseFeedBacks` catalog. `get_license_images` is images only. So for the licensing mirror, we'd still need to add a `get_licensing_status` (+ `get_feedback_catalog`) tool.
❌ **Not "everything"** — it's a hand-picked set of existing API endpoints, with **no ad-hoc query** escape hatch. It can't reach a module until someone adds a tool for it.
⚠️ **Different architecture than the brief I wrote.** It proxies **APIs with a user JWT**, whereas my brief proposed a **SELECT-only DB role on a read replica + `run_select`**. Consequence: here "read-only" rests on *only exposing read tools* (and the JWT's API permissions) — not a structural read-only database role. And it has **none** of the brief's governance yet: no scoped/revocable service tokens, no pricing/PII redaction, no audit log, no rate limiting.

## Is it wired up? — **No.**
- **Not deployed.** `docs/deployment.md` says it plainly: "Dockerfile but **no configured deployment pipeline**." No `cloudbuild.yaml`, no GitHub Actions, no Cloud Run URL / GCP project defined. So it won't auto-deploy on push like your other services.
- **Points at `localhost`** (the BFF at `localhost:5004`) by default — wired for *local dev*, not sandbox or prod.
- **Inert without a JWT** — `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT` is empty by default; without it, only the docs tools work.
- **Not connected to any AI client** — it's not in this session's MCP list and there's no client config in the repo.
- **Early scaffold** — 3 commits, last touched **2026-06-08**, v0.1.0.

## What I'd do
It's a genuinely good starting point — **extend it, don't restart.** Three steps:
1. **Add the licensing tools** (`get_licensing_status`, `get_feedback_catalog`) — this is precisely the poppim sync's data source, and it slots into the existing pattern.
2. **Wire it up** — add a deploy path (Cloud Build → Cloud Run), point it at the **sandbox** BFF, give it a dedicated **service** JWT (not your personal one), and register it with your AI clients.
3. **Add the governance** from the brief for production — a read-only *service account* instead of a write-capable user JWT, audit logging, rate limits, and pricing/PII handling.

One reconciliation to decide: this MCP is **API-proxy**; my brief was **direct-DB read replica**. For true "read-only access to *everything*," you'd likely want the brief's `run_select`-over-`df_ro` layer *as well*, for ad-hoc reads beyond the curated tools. So the real question is whether to (a) grow this API-proxy MCP tool-by-tool, or (b) also stand up the direct-DB read layer — or both.

Want me to (1) add the licensing tools to this MCP and wire up a deploy, or (2) update the access brief so it reconciles with this existing repo (API-proxy + optional direct-DB layer)?

1	# AGENTS.md - Designflow MCP Operating Guide
2	
3	## Project summary
4	
5	Designflow MCP is a TypeScript/Node MCP server for AI clients that need Designflow company context, API documentation, and read-only lookups into the existing Designflow BFF/backend services. Future developers and AI sessions use it to discover a small set of always-on MCP tools, search a hidden operation registry, and query Designflow without loading a huge `tools/list` into every session.
6	
7	## Multi-model AI note
8	
9	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
10	
11	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
12	
13	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
14	
15	## Documentation map: what to read for each task
16	
17	Always start with:
18	
19	- `AGENTS.md`
20	
21	Then load additional docs only when relevant:
22	
23	| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
24	|---|---|---|
25	| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Deep docs under `docs/` unless task requires them |
26	| Modify app behavior or project-owned code | `AGENTS.md`, relevant folder-level `README.md`, `docs/architecture.md` if system design is affected | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
27	| Add or change configuration, env vars, feature flags, secrets, or runtime settings | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime env is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |
28	| Change local setup, dev scripts, test/lint/debug workflow, package scripts, or tooling | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md`, relevant package/config files | `docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
29	| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, hosting, release flow, rollback, or runtime environment | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, relevant workflow/deployment files | Local-only development docs unless needed |
30	| Change database schema, migrations, models, external IDs, or data flow | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env/config is affected, relevant migration/model docs | Deployment docs unless rollout/deploy behavior changes |
31	| Investigate bugs or incidents | `AGENTS.md`, relevant docs based on affected area, `HANDOFF.md` if present, Critical incidents section in `AGENTS.md` | Unrelated folder-level READMEs |
32	| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, relevant docs named inside `HANDOFF.md` | Docs unrelated to the handoff scope |
33	| Work in a subfolder with its own README | `AGENTS.md`, that folder-level `README.md`, and only broader docs referenced there | Other folder-level READMEs |
34	| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | Other docs unless task requires them |
35	| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected docs under `docs/`, folder-level READMEs only where relevant | Source files except as needed to verify accuracy |
36	
37	Rules:
38	
39	- Keep this map task-based.
40	- Do not make future sessions read every Markdown file.
41	- If `HANDOFF.md` exists, read it for continuation work.
42	- Update this map when documentation files are added, removed, renamed, or repurposed.
43	
44	## Repository structure
45	
46	Project-owned code:
47	
48	- `src/index.ts` - process entry point, transport selection, Express HTTP server, stdio startup.
49	- `src/server.ts` - MCP server factory and the always-on MCP tool registrations.
50	- `src/clients/designflow-client.ts` - HTTP client for existing Designflow BFF/backend services.
51	- `src/config/settings.ts` - environment and `tools-config.json` loading.
52	- `src/registry/` - hidden capability registry, search, grouping, doc loading, and metadata.
53	- `src/types.ts` - shared TypeScript types.
54	- `tests/unit/` - Vitest unit tests for registry, config, and visible MCP tool-surface behavior.
55	- `tools-config.json` - hidden capability enablement.
56	
57	Generated code and build artifacts:
58	
59	- `dist/` - TypeScript build output from `npm run build`; do not edit.
60	- `package-lock.json` - generated dependency lockfile; update through `npm install`.
61	
62	Third-party/vendor/framework code:
63	
64	- `node_modules/` - installed npm packages; never edit or load into AI context.
65	
66	Docs:
67	
68	- `README.md` - quick entry point and orientation.
69	- `AGENTS.md` - canonical AI/developer operating guide and doc router.
70	- `CLAUDE.md` - Claude Code-specific instructions only.
71	- `docs/architecture.md` - system design, components, data flow, constraints.
72	- `docs/development.md` - local setup, run/test/debug workflow.
73	- `docs/configuration.md` - environment variables, config files, feature flags.
74	- `docs/deployment.md` - real deployment status, Docker image build path, and unknowns.
75	
76	Scripts and tooling:
77	
78	- `package.json` - npm scripts and dependency inventory.
79	- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript compiler settings.
80	
81	Migrations:
82	
83	- None. This service has no database and no migration system.
84	
85	Deployment files:
86	
87	- `Dockerfile` - production image build for the MCP service.
88	- `.dockerignore` - Docker build-context exclusions.
89	
90	## Prime Directive: custom-code boundary
91	
92	Our custom code lives here:
93	
94	- `src/index.ts`
95	- `src/server.ts`
96	- `src/clients/`
97	- `src/config/`
98	- `src/registry/`
99	- `src/types.ts`
100	- `tests/`
101	- `docs/`
102	- `README.md`
103	- `AGENTS.md`
104	- `CLAUDE.md`
105	- `tools-config.json`
106	- `package.json`
107	- `tsconfig.json`
108	- `Dockerfile`
109	- `.dockerignore`
110	- `.gitignore`
111	- `.claudeignore`
112	- `.cursorignore`
113	
114	Everything else requires justification before touching.
115	
116	Do not put Designflow business logic into third-party packages, generated build output, or random new framework directories. The MCP service should remain a thin lazy-discovery layer over existing Designflow APIs and docs.
117	
118	## Core modification inventory
119	
120	| File | Change made | Why it was necessary | Risk during upgrades |
121	|---|---|---|---|
122	| n/a | No files outside project-owned areas have been modified. | n/a | n/a |
123	
124	## Task-to-file navigation: what to edit for common changes
125	
126	| Task | Files to touch | Files not to touch |
127	|---|---|---|
128	| Add a read-only hidden MCP capability | `src/registry/tool-defs.ts`, `src/registry/groups.ts` if a new group/keyword is needed, `tools-config.json`, tests if behavior is added | `src/server.ts` unless adding an always-on tool is explicitly justified |
129	| Add an always-on MCP tool | `src/server.ts`, `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, tests | `src/registry/tool-defs.ts` alone; hidden operations must not become always-on accidentally |
130	| Change capability search behavior | `src/registry/catalog.ts`, `src/registry/groups.ts`, tests | API client files unless service calls changed |
131	| Change Designflow API base URLs or auth headers | `src/clients/designflow-client.ts`, `src/config/settings.ts`, `.env.example`, `docs/configuration.md` | Hidden tool definitions unless endpoint contracts changed |
132	| Add a documentation-backed resource/tool | `src/registry/doc-reader.ts`, `src/registry/tool-defs.ts`, `tools-config.json`, `docs/architecture.md` if data flow changes | `node_modules/`, `dist/` |
133	| Change local run/build workflow | `package.json`, `docs/development.md`, `README.md` | `docs/deployment.md` unless deployment behavior changes |
134	| Change Docker image build/runtime | `Dockerfile`, `.dockerignore`, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env vars change | Local-only dev docs unless scripts change |
135	| Add write/destructive operations | `src/registry/tool-defs.ts`, `src/server.ts` only if confirmation enforcement changes, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, tests | Do not enable writes in `tools-config.json` without preview/confirmation/audit behavior |
136	| Add or change ignore rules | `.gitignore`, `.dockerignore` where relevant, `.claudeignore`, `.cursorignore`, `AGENTS.md` What to ignore | Source files unless generated/build paths changed |
137	
138	## Data model and external identifiers
139	
140	| Entity/System | Identifier | Where defined | Notes |
141	|---|---|---|---|
142	| MCP server name | `designflow-mcp` | `src/server.ts`, `package.json` | Name clients see during MCP initialization. |
143	| MCP version | `0.1.0` | `package.json`, `src/server.ts` | Keep in sync when publishing versioned releases. |
144	| HTTP MCP endpoint | `POST /mcp` | `src/index.ts` | Stateless Streamable HTTP endpoint. |
145	| HTTP health endpoint | `GET /health` | `src/index.ts` | Returns service status JSON. |
146	| Default HTTP port | `3010` | `src/index.ts`, `.env.example`, `docs/configuration.md` | Override with `MCP_PORT`. |
147	| Core API base | `DESIGNFLOW_CORE_BASE_URL` | `.env.example`, `src/config/settings.ts` | Defaults to `http://localhost:5004/api/core`. |
148	| Item Master API base | `DESIGNFLOW_ITEM_MASTER_BASE_URL` | `.env.example`, `src/config/settings.ts` | Defaults to `http://localhost:5004/api/item_master`. |
149	| Tracking API base | `DESIGNFLOW_TRACKING_BASE_URL` | `.env.example`, `src/config/settings.ts` | Defaults to `http://localhost:5004/api/tracking`. |
150	| Data Sync API base | `DESIGNFLOW_DATA_SYNC_BASE_URL` | `.env.example`, `src/config/settings.ts` | Defaults to `http://localhost:5004/api/data_sync`. |
151	| User JWT env var | `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT` | `.env.example`, `src/clients/designflow-client.ts` | Optional for docs-only tools; required for user-scoped API tools. Secret value must not be committed. |
152	| Data Sync API key env var | `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY` | `.env.example`, `src/clients/designflow-client.ts` | Required only for data-sync backed tools. Secret value must not be committed. |
153	| Hidden capability config | `tools-config.json` | repo root, `src/config/settings.ts` | Controls enabled hidden tools without code changes. |
154	| Always-on tools | `health`, `list_capabilities`, `get_capability_details`, `tool_search`, `invoke_tool` | `src/server.ts` | Keep this list intentionally small. |
155	
156	## Container and service inventory
157	
158	| Container/service | Purpose | Managed by | App/project ID | Image/source |
159	|---|---|---|---|---|
160	| `designflow-mcp` Docker image | Runs the MCP HTTP server with `node dist/index.js --http` | `Dockerfile`; no deployed runtime found in this repo | Unknown; no Cloud Run service or GitHub Actions workflow is present in this repo | Built from `node:22-slim` and this repo source |
161	
162	No database, queue, worker, or sidecar services are defined in this repo.
163	
164	## What to ignore
165	
166	Do not spend AI context on:
167	
168	- `node_modules/`
169	- `dist/`
170	- `.cache/`
171	- `coverage/`
172	- `.env`
173	- `.env.*` except `.env.example`
174	- npm debug logs
175	
176	These entries must stay aligned across `.gitignore`, `.claudeignore`, `.cursorignore`, and the relevant Docker exclusions in `.dockerignore`.
177	
178	## Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
179	
180	### Lazy capability registry
181	
182	Looks like:
183	The server only exposes five MCP tools even though `tools-config.json` enables many Designflow operations.
184	
185	Actually:
186	Only `health`, `list_capabilities`, `get_capability_details`, `tool_search`, and `invoke_tool` are registered with MCP. Real operations live in `src/registry/tool-defs.ts` and are executed by `invoke_tool`.
187	
188	Why:
189	Large eager `tools/list` payloads waste session context and can degrade AI behavior. Returning operation contracts as text through discovery tools keeps the initial MCP surface small.
190	
191	Do not change because:
192	Registering every Designflow operation directly would recreate the context-bloat problem this service is designed to avoid.
193	
194	### No dynamic tool registration
195	
196	Looks like:
197	The service ignores MCP `tools/list_changed` as a way to register matching tools after search.
198	
199	Actually:
200	Discovery tools return structured contracts as text, and `invoke_tool` executes by exact operation name.
201	
202	Why:
203	Real clients may cache the initial `tools/list` and not reliably refetch after notifications.
204	
205	Do not change because:
206	Dynamic registration can add complexity without delivering durable context savings.
207	
208	### Stateless HTTP transport
209	
210	Looks like:
211	`GET /mcp` and `DELETE /mcp` return `405`, and each `POST /mcp` creates a new server/transport.
212	
213	Actually:
214	The HTTP mode is a stateless Streamable HTTP implementation using `sessionIdGenerator: undefined`.
215	
216	Why:
217	The current tool set is read-only and does not require server-initiated notifications or resumable sessions. Stateless mode is simpler for a future Cloud Run deployment.
218	
219	Do not change because:
220	Adding session state increases operational complexity. If stateful features become necessary, preserve the lazy registry and document the reason.
221	
222	### Documentation tools read sibling repos
223	
224	Looks like:
225	`src/registry/doc-reader.ts` reaches outside this repo to `../designflow-*` paths.
226	
227	Actually:
228	Doc-backed hidden tools read the API reference files in sibling Designflow repos checked out under `D:\repos\dflow`.
229	
230	Why:
231	The MCP server is intended to help AI sessions understand the whole Designflow system, not only this service.
232	
233	Do not change because:
234	Replacing this with copied docs would create stale duplicates. If the workspace layout changes, update `DOC_PATHS` and document the new layout.
235	
236	## Credentials and environment
237	
238	| Variable | Purpose | Stored where | Required in dev | Required in prod |
239	|---|---|---|---|---|
240	| `MCP_PORT` | HTTP server port for `--http` mode | `.env`, Cloud Run/service env if deployed | no; defaults to `3010` | yes if deployed port differs from `3010` |
241	| `DESIGNFLOW_CORE_BASE_URL` | Base URL for core Designflow API calls | `.env`, runtime env | no; defaults to local BFF core URL | yes when deployed |
242	| `DESIGNFLOW_ITEM_MASTER_BASE_URL` | Base URL for item-master API calls | `.env`, runtime env | no; defaults to local BFF item-master URL | yes when deployed |
243	| `DESIGNFLOW_TRACKING_BASE_URL` | Base URL for tracking API calls | `.env`, runtime env | no; defaults to local BFF tracking URL | yes when deployed |
244	| `DESIGNFLOW_DATA_SYNC_BASE_URL` | Base URL for data-sync API calls | `.env`, runtime env | no; defaults to local BFF data-sync URL | yes when deployed |
245	| `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT` | Optional user JWT for user-scoped Designflow API tools | `.env`, secret/runtime env | only for API-backed tools | yes for user-scoped deployment; storage mechanism unknown |
246	| `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY` | Optional data-sync API key | `.env`, secret/runtime env | only for data-sync tools | yes if data-sync tools are enabled in production |
247	| `DESIGNFLOW_MCP_ENABLED_TOOLS` | Comma-separated extra hidden tools to enable | `.env`, runtime env | no | no |
248	| `DESIGNFLOW_MCP_DISABLED_TOOLS` | Comma-separated hidden tools to disable | `.env`, runtime env | no | no |
249	| `NODE_ENV` | Runtime mode in Docker image | `Dockerfile`, runtime env | no | set to `production` by Dockerfile |
250	
251	Do not commit secret values. `.env.example` must contain names and safe defaults only.
252	
253	## Deployment
254	
255	Current real deployment status:
256	
257	- No GitHub Actions workflow exists in this repo.
258	- No `cloudbuild.yaml` exists in this repo.
259	- No Cloud Run service name, GCP project ID, or deployed URL is defined in this repo.
260	- `Dockerfile` can build a production image that runs `node dist/index.js --http`.
261	- Runtime environment variables would need to be supplied by the future deployment platform.
262	- SSH deployment is not documented and is not a normal deployment path for this repo.
263	
264	Known image build path:
265	
266	```bash
267	docker build -t designflow-mcp .
268	docker run --rm -p 3010:3010 --env-file .env designflow-mcp
269	```
270	
271	Rollback method:
272	
273	- Unknown because no deployment platform is configured in this repo.
274	- Verify by checking for future `.github/workflows/`, `cloudbuild.yaml`, infrastructure code, or the deployment platform dashboard.
275	
276	Deploy trigger:
277	
278	- Unknown/not configured in this repo.
279	
280	## Critical incidents
281	
282	No production incidents are known for this repo. This service has not been documented as deployed.
283	
284	Potential risk already addressed:
285	
286	### 2026-06-08 Tools-list context bloat avoided
287	
288	What happened:
289	This service was scaffolded after another MCP server showed that eager registration of many tool schemas can consume substantial session context.
290	
291	Impact:
292	No Designflow production impact. The lesson shaped this initial architecture.
293	
294	Root cause:
295	Large MCP `tools/list` surfaces are expensive for AI sessions.
296	
297	Recovery:
298	Designflow MCP was built with a lazy registry and five always-on tools.
299	
300	Rule added to prevent recurrence:
301	Do not eagerly register hidden Designflow operations. Use discovery tools plus `invoke_tool`.
302	
303	## Pending work
304	
305	| Status | Item | Owner/next action |
306	|---|---|---|
307	| open | Create/attach GitHub repository and remote | Create `popcre/designflow-mcp` or confirm target repo, then push `sandbox-albert`. |
308	| open | Configure production deployment | Add the real deployment workflow/platform details after the service is deployed. |
309	| open | Replace static `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT` production auth | Decide user-scoped vs service-scoped auth for remote MCP clients before production. |
310	| done | Add unit tests for registry/search behavior and visible MCP tool surface | Completed with `tests/unit/catalog.test.ts`, `tests/unit/settings.test.ts`, and `tests/unit/server.test.ts`. Add `DesignflowClient` header tests before expanding API client behavior. |

# AGENTS.md - Designflow MCP Operating Guide

## Project summary

Designflow MCP is a TypeScript/Node MCP server for AI clients that need Designflow company context, API documentation, and read-only lookups into the existing Designflow BFF/backend services. Future developers and AI sessions use it to discover a small set of always-on MCP tools, search a hidden operation registry, and query Designflow without loading a huge `tools/list` into every session.

## Multi-model AI note

There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.

`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.

When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.

## Documentation map: what to read for each task

Always start with:

- `AGENTS.md`

Then load additional docs only when relevant:

| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
|---|---|---|
| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Deep docs under `docs/` unless task requires them |
| Modify app behavior or project-owned code | `AGENTS.md`, relevant folder-level `README.md`, `docs/architecture.md` if system design is affected | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
| Add or change configuration, env vars, feature flags, secrets, or runtime settings | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime env is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |
| Change local setup, dev scripts, test/lint/debug workflow, package scripts, or tooling | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md`, relevant package/config files | `docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, hosting, release flow, rollback, or runtime environment | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, relevant workflow/deployment files | Local-only development docs unless needed |
| Change database schema, migrations, models, external IDs, or data flow | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env/config is affected, relevant migration/model docs | Deployment docs unless rollout/deploy behavior changes |
| Investigate bugs or incidents | `AGENTS.md`, relevant docs based on affected area, `HANDOFF.md` if present, Critical incidents section in `AGENTS.md` | Unrelated folder-level READMEs |
| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, relevant docs named inside `HANDOFF.md` | Docs unrelated to the handoff scope |
| Work in a subfolder with its own README | `AGENTS.md`, that folder-level `README.md`, and only broader docs referenced there | Other folder-level READMEs |
| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | Other docs unless task requires them |
| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected docs under `docs/`, folder-level READMEs only where relevant | Source files except as needed to verify accuracy |

Rules:

- Keep this map task-based.
- Do not make future sessions read every Markdown file.
- If `HANDOFF.md` exists, read it for continuation work.
- Update this map when documentation files are added, removed, renamed, or repurposed.

## Repository structure

Project-owned code:

- `src/index.ts` - process entry point, transport selection, Express HTTP server, stdio startup.
- `src/server.ts` - MCP server factory and the always-on MCP tool registrations.
- `src/clients/designflow-client.ts` - HTTP client for existing Designflow BFF/backend services.
- `src/config/settings.ts` - environment and `tools-config.json` loading.
- `src/registry/` - hidden capability registry, search, grouping, doc loading, and metadata.
- `src/types.ts` - shared TypeScript types.
- `tests/unit/` - Vitest unit tests for registry, config, and visible MCP tool-surface behavior.
- `tools-config.json` - hidden capability enablement.

Generated code and build artifacts:

- `dist/` - TypeScript build output from `npm run build`; do not edit.
- `package-lock.json` - generated dependency lockfile; update through `npm install`.

Third-party/vendor/framework code:

- `node_modules/` - installed npm packages; never edit or load into AI context.

Docs:

- `README.md` - quick entry point and orientation.
- `AGENTS.md` - canonical AI/developer operating guide and doc router.
- `CLAUDE.md` - Claude Code-specific instructions only.
- `docs/architecture.md` - system design, components, data flow, constraints.
- `docs/development.md` - local setup, run/test/debug workflow.
- `docs/configuration.md` - environment variables, config files, feature flags.
- `docs/deployment.md` - real deployment status, Docker image build path, and unknowns.

Scripts and tooling:

- `package.json` - npm scripts and dependency inventory.
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript compiler settings.

Migrations:

- None. This service has no database and no migration system.

Deployment files:

- `Dockerfile` - production image build for the MCP service.
- `.dockerignore` - Docker build-context exclusions.

## Prime Directive: custom-code boundary

Our custom code lives here:

- `src/index.ts`
- `src/server.ts`
- `src/clients/`
- `src/config/`
- `src/registry/`
- `src/types.ts`
- `tests/`
- `docs/`
- `README.md`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- `tools-config.json`
- `package.json`
- `tsconfig.json`
- `Dockerfile`
- `.dockerignore`
- `.gitignore`
- `.claudeignore`
- `.cursorignore`

Everything else requires justification before touching.

Do not put Designflow business logic into third-party packages, generated build output, or random new framework directories. The MCP service should remain a thin lazy-discovery layer over existing Designflow APIs and docs.

## Core modification inventory

| File | Change made | Why it was necessary | Risk during upgrades |
|---|---|---|---|
| n/a | No files outside project-owned areas have been modified. | n/a | n/a |

## Task-to-file navigation: what to edit for common changes

| Task | Files to touch | Files not to touch |
|---|---|---|
| Add a read-only hidden MCP capability | `src/registry/tool-defs.ts`, `src/registry/groups.ts` if a new group/keyword is needed, `tools-config.json`, tests if behavior is added | `src/server.ts` unless adding an always-on tool is explicitly justified |
| Add an always-on MCP tool | `src/server.ts`, `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, tests | `src/registry/tool-defs.ts` alone; hidden operations must not become always-on accidentally |
| Change capability search behavior | `src/registry/catalog.ts`, `src/registry/groups.ts`, tests | API client files unless service calls changed |
| Change Designflow API base URLs or auth headers | `src/clients/designflow-client.ts`, `src/config/settings.ts`, `.env.example`, `docs/configuration.md` | Hidden tool definitions unless endpoint contracts changed |
| Add a documentation-backed resource/tool | `src/registry/doc-reader.ts`, `src/registry/tool-defs.ts`, `tools-config.json`, `docs/architecture.md` if data flow changes | `node_modules/`, `dist/` |
| Change local run/build workflow | `package.json`, `docs/development.md`, `README.md` | `docs/deployment.md` unless deployment behavior changes |
| Change Docker image build/runtime | `Dockerfile`, `.dockerignore`, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env vars change | Local-only dev docs unless scripts change |
| Add write/destructive operations | `src/registry/tool-defs.ts`, `src/server.ts` only if confirmation enforcement changes, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, tests | Do not enable writes in `tools-config.json` without preview/confirmation/audit behavior |
| Add or change ignore rules | `.gitignore`, `.dockerignore` where relevant, `.claudeignore`, `.cursorignore`, `AGENTS.md` What to ignore | Source files unless generated/build paths changed |

## Data model and external identifiers

| Entity/System | Identifier | Where defined | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| MCP server name | `designflow-mcp` | `src/server.ts`, `package.json` | Name clients see during MCP initialization. |
| MCP version | `0.1.0` | `package.json`, `src/server.ts` | Keep in sync when publishing versioned releases. |
| HTTP MCP endpoint | `POST /mcp` | `src/index.ts` | Stateless Streamable HTTP endpoint. |
| HTTP health endpoint | `GET /health` | `src/index.ts` | Returns service status JSON. |
| Default HTTP port | `3010` | `src/index.ts`, `.env.example`, `docs/configuration.md` | Override with `MCP_PORT`. |
| Core API base | `DESIGNFLOW_CORE_BASE_URL` | `.env.example`, `src/config/settings.ts` | Defaults to `http://localhost:5004/api/core`. |
| Item Master API base | `DESIGNFLOW_ITEM_MASTER_BASE_URL` | `.env.example`, `src/config/settings.ts` | Defaults to `http://localhost:5004/api/item_master`. |
| Tracking API base | `DESIGNFLOW_TRACKING_BASE_URL` | `.env.example`, `src/config/settings.ts` | Defaults to `http://localhost:5004/api/tracking`. |
| Data Sync API base | `DESIGNFLOW_DATA_SYNC_BASE_URL` | `.env.example`, `src/config/settings.ts` | Defaults to `http://localhost:5004/api/data_sync`. |
| User JWT env var | `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT` | `.env.example`, `src/clients/designflow-client.ts` | Optional for docs-only tools; required for user-scoped API tools. Secret value must not be committed. |
| Data Sync API key env var | `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY` | `.env.example`, `src/clients/designflow-client.ts` | Required only for data-sync backed tools. Secret value must not be committed. |
| Hidden capability config | `tools-config.json` | repo root, `src/config/settings.ts` | Controls enabled hidden tools without code changes. |
| Always-on tools | `health`, `list_capabilities`, `get_capability_details`, `tool_search`, `invoke_tool` | `src/server.ts` | Keep this list intentionally small. |

## Container and service inventory

| Container/service | Purpose | Managed by | App/project ID | Image/source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `designflow-mcp` Docker image | Runs the MCP HTTP server with `node dist/index.js --http` | `Dockerfile`; no deployed runtime found in this repo | Unknown; no Cloud Run service or GitHub Actions workflow is present in this repo | Built from `node:22-slim` and this repo source |

No database, queue, worker, or sidecar services are defined in this repo.

## What to ignore

Do not spend AI context on:

- `node_modules/`
- `dist/`
- `.cache/`
- `coverage/`
- `.env`
- `.env.*` except `.env.example`
- npm debug logs

These entries must stay aligned across `.gitignore`, `.claudeignore`, `.cursorignore`, and the relevant Docker exclusions in `.dockerignore`.

## Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions

### Lazy capability registry

Looks like:
The server only exposes five MCP tools even though `tools-config.json` enables many Designflow operations.

Actually:
Only `health`, `list_capabilities`, `get_capability_details`, `tool_search`, and `invoke_tool` are registered with MCP. Real operations live in `src/registry/tool-defs.ts` and are executed by `invoke_tool`.

Why:
Large eager `tools/list` payloads waste session context and can degrade AI behavior. Returning operation contracts as text through discovery tools keeps the initial MCP surface small.

Do not change because:
Registering every Designflow operation directly would recreate the context-bloat problem this service is designed to avoid.

### No dynamic tool registration

Looks like:
The service ignores MCP `tools/list_changed` as a way to register matching tools after search.

Actually:
Discovery tools return structured contracts as text, and `invoke_tool` executes by exact operation name.

Why:
Real clients may cache the initial `tools/list` and not reliably refetch after notifications.

Do not change because:
Dynamic registration can add complexity without delivering durable context savings.

### Stateless HTTP transport

Looks like:
`GET /mcp` and `DELETE /mcp` return `405`, and each `POST /mcp` creates a new server/transport.

Actually:
The HTTP mode is a stateless Streamable HTTP implementation using `sessionIdGenerator: undefined`.

Why:
The current tool set is read-only and does not require server-initiated notifications or resumable sessions. Stateless mode is simpler for a future Cloud Run deployment.

Do not change because:
Adding session state increases operational complexity. If stateful features become necessary, preserve the lazy registry and document the reason.

### Documentation tools read sibling repos

Looks like:
`src/registry/doc-reader.ts` reaches outside this repo to `../designflow-*` paths.

Actually:
Doc-backed hidden tools read the API reference files in sibling Designflow repos checked out under `D:\repos\dflow`.

Why:
The MCP server is intended to help AI sessions understand the whole Designflow system, not only this service.

Do not change because:
Replacing this with copied docs would create stale duplicates. If the workspace layout changes, update `DOC_PATHS` and document the new layout.

## Credentials and environment

| Variable | Purpose | Stored where | Required in dev | Required in prod |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `MCP_PORT` | HTTP server port for `--http` mode | `.env`, Cloud Run/service env if deployed | no; defaults to `3010` | yes if deployed port differs from `3010` |
| `DESIGNFLOW_CORE_BASE_URL` | Base URL for core Designflow API calls | `.env`, runtime env | no; defaults to local BFF core URL | yes when deployed |
| `DESIGNFLOW_ITEM_MASTER_BASE_URL` | Base URL for item-master API calls | `.env`, runtime env | no; defaults to local BFF item-master URL | yes when deployed |
| `DESIGNFLOW_TRACKING_BASE_URL` | Base URL for tracking API calls | `.env`, runtime env | no; defaults to local BFF tracking URL | yes when deployed |
| `DESIGNFLOW_DATA_SYNC_BASE_URL` | Base URL for data-sync API calls | `.env`, runtime env | no; defaults to local BFF data-sync URL | yes when deployed |
| `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT` | Optional user JWT for user-scoped Designflow API tools | `.env`, secret/runtime env | only for API-backed tools | yes for user-scoped deployment; storage mechanism unknown |
| `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY` | Optional data-sync API key | `.env`, secret/runtime env | only for data-sync tools | yes if data-sync tools are enabled in production |
| `DESIGNFLOW_MCP_ENABLED_TOOLS` | Comma-separated extra hidden tools to enable | `.env`, runtime env | no | no |
| `DESIGNFLOW_MCP_DISABLED_TOOLS` | Comma-separated hidden tools to disable | `.env`, runtime env | no | no |
| `NODE_ENV` | Runtime mode in Docker image | `Dockerfile`, runtime env | no | set to `production` by Dockerfile |

Do not commit secret values. `.env.example` must contain names and safe defaults only.

## Deployment

Current real deployment status:

- No GitHub Actions workflow exists in this repo.
- No `cloudbuild.yaml` exists in this repo.
- No Cloud Run service name, GCP project ID, or deployed URL is defined in this repo.
- `Dockerfile` can build a production image that runs `node dist/index.js --http`.
- Runtime environment variables would need to be supplied by the future deployment platform.
- SSH deployment is not documented and is not a normal deployment path for this repo.

Known image build path:

```bash
docker build -t designflow-mcp .
docker run --rm -p 3010:3010 --env-file .env designflow-mcp
```

Rollback method:

- Unknown because no deployment platform is configured in this repo.
- Verify by checking for future `.github/workflows/`, `cloudbuild.yaml`, infrastructure code, or the deployment platform dashboard.

Deploy trigger:

- Unknown/not configured in this repo.

## Critical incidents

No production incidents are known for this repo. This service has not been documented as deployed.

Potential risk already addressed:

### 2026-06-08 Tools-list context bloat avoided

What happened:
This service was scaffolded after another MCP server showed that eager registration of many tool schemas can consume substantial session context.

Impact:
No Designflow production impact. The lesson shaped this initial architecture.

Root cause:
Large MCP `tools/list` surfaces are expensive for AI sessions.

Recovery:
Designflow MCP was built with a lazy registry and five always-on tools.

Rule added to prevent recurrence:
Do not eagerly register hidden Designflow operations. Use discovery tools plus `invoke_tool`.

## Pending work

| Status | Item | Owner/next action |
|---|---|---|
| open | Create/attach GitHub repository and remote | Create `popcre/designflow-mcp` or confirm target repo, then push `sandbox-albert`. |
| open | Configure production deployment | Add the real deployment workflow/platform details after the service is deployed. |
| open | Replace static `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT` production auth | Decide user-scoped vs service-scoped auth for remote MCP clients before production. |
| done | Add unit tests for registry/search behavior and visible MCP tool surface | Completed with `tests/unit/catalog.test.ts`, `tests/unit/settings.test.ts`, and `tests/unit/server.test.ts`. Add `DesignflowClient` header tests before expanding API client behavior. |

input.content:
# Architecture Update & Implementation Plan — Designflow MCP

**Status:** plan. Target: take this v0.1.0 read-only scaffold to a deployed, authenticated, broadly-capable
read-only access layer for AI sessions across the whole Designflow application.
**Read first:** `AGENTS.md` (canonical), `docs/architecture.md`. This plan extends, it does not replace them.

---

## 1. Where we are

A TypeScript MCP server with a **lazy registry**: five always-on tools (`health`, `list_capabilities`,
`get_capability_details`, `tool_search`, `invoke_tool`) and a hidden catalog of read-only operations
(`src/registry/tool-defs.ts`, enabled via `tools-config.json`). It reaches Designflow data by calling the
**BFF/back-end HTTP APIs** through `DesignflowClient`, authenticated with a **single `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT`**.
All real ops are safety **tier 1**; `invoke_tool` rejects tier > 1, so writes are structurally off.

**Gaps this plan closes (two already flagged in `AGENTS.md` → Pending work):**
1. **No licensing-status capability** — nothing reads `licensingStatus` / Brand Assurance / the
   `LicenseFeedBacks` catalog. This is the immediate blocker for the poppim licensing mirror.
2. **Not deployed and the HTTP transport is unauthenticated** — `POST /mcp` has no inbound auth; once on
   Cloud Run it would be open to anyone who can reach it.
3. **Outbound auth is a personal user JWT** — should be a dedicated read-only service principal.
4. **Narrow coverage** — curated subset of endpoints; no production tracking, merch-group/licensor
   resolution, customers, RFQ groups, or ad-hoc reads; no field-level protection for pricing/PII.

---

## 2. Goals

- Give AI sessions safe, **authenticated, read-only** access to **all major Designflow domains** through
  one discoverable service.
- Keep the lazy-registry design (do not bloat `tools/list`).
- Make "read-only" a **layered guarantee** (service principal + tier-1-only + redaction), not just a
  convention.
- Get it **deployed and wired up** through the existing push→Cloud Build→Cloud Run model, pointed at
  **sandbox** first.
- Specifically unblock the **poppim licensing mirror** (its read source becomes a tool here, not a bespoke
  endpoint).

---

## 3. Cross-cutting architecture changes

### 3.1 Inbound auth on the MCP transport (P0 — must land with first deploy)
Today `POST /mcp` is open. Add a bearer-token gate in `src/index.ts` (Express middleware): require
`Authorization: Bearer <consumer-token>`; reject otherwise. Tokens are **per-consumer, scoped, revocable**,
loaded from env/Secret Manager (e.g. `MCP_CLIENT_TOKENS` = id→token→scopes map). `/health` stays open.
*Do not deploy publicly without this.*

### 3.2 Outbound auth: read-only service principal (closes AGENTS pending #309)
Replace the personal `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT` with a **dedicated Designflow service account** whose API
permissions are **read-only**. Even though only tier-1 tools are exposed, the outbound credential itself
must not be write-capable. Keep the env var name but document that prod must use the service principal, not
a human's token.

### 3.3 Field redaction + scopes (pricing/PII)
Add a small **policy layer** applied to tool output in `DesignflowClient`/a response filter:
- Default-**redact** pricing/cost/margin/royalty fields (RFQ + licensing costing) and PII (emails, phones,
  addresses).
- Reveal them only when the consumer token carries an explicit `pricing` / `pii` scope.
- Drive it from a version-controlled `policy` (deny columns, scope map) so the surface is reviewable.
This mirrors the business rule that pricing stays hidden from most roles.

### 3.4 Audit logging
Log every `invoke_tool` call: consumer-token id, tool name, args (redacted), upstream status, duration,
timestamp. Start with structured stdout (Cloud Logging captures it); optionally a dedicated sink later.

### 3.5 Rate limiting, pagination, response caps
Per-consumer rate limit on `POST /mcp`; standard `limit`/`offset` args + a hard max page size and max
response bytes on list tools, so a single call can't pull unbounded data.

### 3.6 Deployment (make it "wired up")
- Add `cloudbuild.yaml` + a **Cloud Build trigger** so it deploys on push like the other services.
- Deploy as a **Cloud Run service** in the Designflow GCP project; env + tokens from **Secret Manager**
  (create the secrets in the same change that wires `--set-secrets`).
- Point base URLs at the **sandbox BFF** first; verify; then production.
- **Register the deployed URL + a client token** with the AI clients (Claude Code MCP config) so sessions
  can actually use it.

### 3.7 (Optional) Direct-DB read layer — reconcile the two architectures
This MCP is **API-proxy**. The companion brief (`designflow-backend/docs/ai-readonly-access-brief.md`)
proposed a **direct read-only DB** layer (`df_ro` role on a replica + a guarded `run_select`). They are
complementary: API-proxy reuses the existing permission model and can't run arbitrary SQL; a guarded
`run_select` covers ad-hoc reads no curated tool exposes. **Decision needed:** grow API-proxy tools only,
or also add a `run_select` tool backed by a `df_ro` replica for true "read-everything." If added, it must
carry every guardrail in the brief (SELECT-only role, deny-lists, redaction, row/time caps, audit).

### 3.8 Safety tiers & future writes
The tier system (1 read / 2 reversible / 3 destructive) is already in `groups.ts` and enforced in
`server.ts`. Keep everything tier 1 for now. A future, explicitly-gated reversible-write path (e.g.
poppim → Designflow status writeback) would add tier-2 tools **with preview/confirmation/audit** — out of
scope here, but the architecture is ready.

---

## 4. New capabilities (tools) to add

Each is a `ToolDef` in `src/registry/tool-defs.ts`, enabled in `tools-config.json`, group from `groups.ts`,
called via `client.request(service, route)`. **"Confirmed"** = endpoint seen in the Designflow code this
session; **"verify"** = endpoint likely exists but confirm route/shape (or add a lean read endpoint).

### 4.1 Licensing (immediate — unblocks the poppim mirror)
| Tool | Service | Route | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `get_licensing_status` | tracking | `/lic/getLic` (filter by item) **or** new lean `/lic/status-export` | confirmed grid / verify lean | latest status + Brand Assurance # + sample # + status history per item |
| `get_feedback_catalog` | core | `/getLicenseFeedBacks` | confirmed | the `LicenseFeedBacks` phase/status catalog (drives any status→stage mapping) |
| `get_licensing_statuses` | tracking | `/lic/statuses` | confirmed | distinct status values |

> A lean **`/lic/status-export`** read endpoint on `designflow-tracking` (returns licensed items with
> `item_num_id, company, division, latest_status, brand_assurance, sample_no, updated_at`) is the cheapest
> backing for `get_licensing_status` and is exactly the read source the poppim sync needs. Building it is
> DesignFlow-side work that ships via the normal push-to-deploy.

### 4.2 Production tracking
| Tool | Service | Route | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `get_prod_orders` / `get_prod_order` | tracking | prod-order header endpoint (`getProdOrderHeader` path) | verify |

### 4.3 Reference data / merch resolution
| Tool | Service | Route | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `get_merch_groups` | core / data_sync | merch-group endpoint | verify | resolve licensor/property/artist codes (types 05/09/15) to names |
| `get_seasons`, `get_product_types`, `get_divisions` | core | reference endpoints | verify | small lookups AI needs constantly |

### 4.4 Customers / buyers / retailers
| Tool | Service | Route | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `search_customers` / `get_customer` | core / data_sync | customers endpoint | verify |

### 4.5 RFQ expansion (pricing-scoped)
| Tool | Service | Route | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `list_rfq_groups` | core | RFQ group list | verify | |
| `get_rfq_vendor_quotes` | core | RFQ vendor quotes | verify | **pricing-scoped** — costs redacted unless `pricing` scope |

### 4.6 Data sync / Coldlion
| Tool | Service | Route | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `get_data_sync_item_headers` | data_sync | item-headers endpoint (needs `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY`) | verify |

### 4.7 Aggregations for dashboards (where endpoints allow)
Counts/rollups (e.g. items by licensing status, samples at factory, prod orders by stage) to answer
operational questions without pulling full lists. Add only where a backing endpoint exists or is added.

---

## 5. How to add a tool (mechanical recipe, per this repo)

1. Add a `ToolDef` to `src/registry/tool-defs.ts` — `name`, `title`, `group`, `safety: SAFETY.readOnly`,
   `description`, `argsDescription`, `argsSchema` (zod), `exampleArgs`, `commonFailures`, `relatedTools`,
   and `invoke: async (args, { client }) => client.request(service, route, { query/body })`.
2. Add the name to `tools-config.json` → `enabled_read_tools`.
3. If a new group/keyword is needed, extend `src/registry/groups.ts` (`TOOL_GROUPS`, `KEYWORD_TO_GROUPS`).
4. Add/extend tests under `tests/unit/` (catalog/search + a client-shape test).
5. `npm run typecheck && npm test`. Keep it tier 1; never enable writes in `tools-config.json` without
   preview/confirmation/audit (per `AGENTS.md`).

---

## 6. Phased roadmap

**Phase 1 — Unblock + deploy (the critical path):**
- Add §4.1 licensing tools (+ the lean `/lic/status-export` endpoint on `designflow-tracking`).
- Add §3.1 inbound auth + §3.2 service-principal outbound auth.
- Add §3.6 `cloudbuild.yaml` + Cloud Run, point at **sandbox** BFF, register with AI clients.
- **Outcome:** AI sessions (and the poppim sync) can pull licensing status through an authenticated,
  deployed MCP. This is the milestone that makes the server real and unblocks the poppim mirror.

**Phase 2 — Breadth + protection:**
- Add §4.2–4.6 tools (prod, merch/reference, customers, RFQ, data-sync).
- Add §3.3 redaction + scopes, §3.4 audit, §3.5 rate limits/pagination.

**Phase 3 — Hardening + reach:**
- Per-consumer scoped tokens, anomaly alerting, promote to production, connect all AI clients.
- Decide §3.7 (add `run_select`/`df_ro` direct-DB layer or stay API-proxy).

**Phase 4 — Later, gated:** reversible tier-2 writes (e.g. status writeback) with preview/confirmation/audit.

---

## 7. Acceptance criteria

- `POST /mcp` rejects unauthenticated/unknown-token requests; `/health` stays open.
- Outbound calls use a read-only service principal; no human JWT in prod.
- `get_licensing_status` returns latest status + Brand Assurance # + sample # for a known item; the poppim
  sync can consume it.
- Pricing/PII fields are absent unless the consumer token carries `pricing`/`pii`.
- Every `invoke_tool` call is audited; a revoked consumer token is denied immediately.
- List tools enforce pagination + max page size; rate limit trips under abuse.
- Deployed to sandbox via Cloud Build trigger; registered and callable from an AI client.

---

## 8. Open decisions

- API-proxy only vs. **also** a `run_select` + `df_ro` direct-DB layer (§3.7).
- Exact **redact** column list (pricing/cost/margin/royalty + PII) — needs a quick policy pass.
- Consumer-token issuance/rotation and where the audit log lives.
- Read replica vs read-only role on primary (only relevant if §3.7 is adopted).

## 9. Ties to other workstreams

- **poppim licensing mirror** (`designflow-frontend/docs/licensing-poppim-phase0-bridge.md`): its permanent
  read access = the §4.1 `get_licensing_status` tool here (backed by `/lic/status-export`). This plan is the
  durable home for that "ongoing read-only access."
- **Read-only access brief** (`designflow-backend/docs/ai-readonly-access-brief.md`): the governance there
  (service principal, redaction, audit, scoped tokens, optional `df_ro`/`run_select`) is folded into §3.
