  convention.
- Get it **deployed and wired up** through the existing push→Cloud Build→Cloud Run model, pointed at
  **sandbox** first.
- Specifically unblock the **poppim licensing mirror** (its read source becomes a tool here, not a bespoke
  endpoint).

---

## 3. Cross-cutting architecture changes

### 3.1 Inbound auth on the MCP transport (P0 — must land with first deploy)
Today `POST /mcp` is open. Add a bearer-token gate in `src/index.ts` (Express middleware): require
`Authorization: Bearer <consumer-token>`; reject otherwise. Tokens are **per-consumer, scoped, revocable**,
loaded from env/Secret Manager (e.g. `MCP_CLIENT_TOKENS` = id→token→scopes map). `/health` stays open.
*Do not deploy publicly without this.*

### 3.2 Outbound auth: read-only service principal (closes AGENTS pending #309)
Replace the personal `DESIGNFLOW_USER_JWT` with a **dedicated Designflow service account** whose API
permissions are **read-only**. Even though only tier-1 tools are exposed, the outbound credential itself
must not be write-capable. Keep the env var name but document that prod must use the service principal, not
a human's token.

### 3.3 Field redaction + scopes (pricing/PII)
Add a small **policy layer** applied to tool output in `DesignflowClient`/a response filter:
- Default-**redact** pricing/cost/margin/royalty fields (RFQ + licensing costing) and PII (emails, phones,
  addresses).
- Reveal them only when the consumer token carries an explicit `pricing` / `pii` scope.
- Drive it from a version-controlled `policy` (deny columns, scope map) so the surface is reviewable.
This mirrors the business rule that pricing stays hidden from most roles.

### 3.4 Audit logging
Log every `invoke_tool` call: consumer-token id, tool name, args (redacted), upstream status, duration,
timestamp. Start with structured stdout (Cloud Logging captures it); optionally a dedicated sink later.

### 3.5 Rate limiting, pagination, response caps
Per-consumer rate limit on `POST /mcp`; standard `limit`/`offset` args + a hard max page size and max
response bytes on list tools, so a single call can't pull unbounded data.

### 3.6 Deployment (make it "wired up")
- Add `cloudbuild.yaml` + a **Cloud Build trigger** so it deploys on push like the other services.
- Deploy as a **Cloud Run service** in the Designflow GCP project; env + tokens from **Secret Manager**
  (create the secrets in the same change that wires `--set-secrets`).
- Point base URLs at the **sandbox BFF** first; verify; then production.
- **Register the deployed URL + a client token** with the AI clients (Claude Code MCP config) so sessions
  can actually use it.

### 3.7 (Optional) Direct-DB read layer — reconcile the two architectures
This MCP is **API-proxy**. The companion brief (`designflow-backend/docs/ai-readonly-access-brief.md`)
proposed a **direct read-only DB** layer (`df_ro` role on a replica + a guarded `run_select`). They are
complementary: API-proxy reuses the existing permission model and can't run arbitrary SQL; a guarded
`run_select` covers ad-hoc reads no curated tool exposes. **Decision needed:** grow API-proxy tools only,
or also add a `run_select` tool backed by a `df_ro` replica for true "read-everything." If added, it must
carry every guardrail in the brief (SELECT-only role, deny-lists, redaction, row/time caps, audit).

### 3.8 Safety tiers & future writes
The tier system (1 read / 2 reversible / 3 destructive) is already in `groups.ts` and enforced in
`server.ts`. Keep everything tier 1 for now. A future, explicitly-gated reversible-write path (e.g.
poppim → Designflow status writeback) would add tier-2 tools **with preview/confirmation/audit** — out of
scope here, but the architecture is ready.

---

## 4. New capabilities (tools) to add

Each is a `ToolDef` in `src/registry/tool-defs.ts`, enabled in `tools-config.json`, group from `groups.ts`,
called via `client.request(service, route)`. **"Confirmed"** = endpoint seen in the Designflow code this
session; **"verify"** = endpoint likely exists but confirm route/shape (or add a lean read endpoint).

### 4.1 Licensing (immediate — unblocks the poppim mirror)
| Tool | Service | Route | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `get_licensing_status` | tracking | `/lic/getLic` (filter by item) **or** new lean `/lic/status-export` | confirmed grid / verify lean | latest status + Brand Assurance # + sample # + status history per item |
| `get_feedback_catalog` | core | `/getLicenseFeedBacks` | confirmed | the `LicenseFeedBacks` phase/status catalog (drives any status→stage mapping) |
| `get_licensing_statuses` | tracking | `/lic/statuses` | confirmed | distinct status values |

> A lean **`/lic/status-export`** read endpoint on `designflow-tracking` (returns licensed items with
> `item_num_id, company, division, latest_status, brand_assurance, sample_no, updated_at`) is the cheapest
> backing for `get_licensing_status` and is exactly the read source the poppim sync needs. Building it is
> DesignFlow-side work that ships via the normal push-to-deploy.

### 4.2 Production tracking
| Tool | Service | Route | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `get_prod_orders` / `get_prod_order` | tracking | prod-order header endpoint (`getProdOrderHeader` path) | verify |

### 4.3 Reference data / merch resolution
| Tool | Service | Route | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `get_merch_groups` | core / data_sync | merch-group endpoint | verify | resolve licensor/property/artist codes (types 05/09/15) to names |
| `get_seasons`, `get_product_types`, `get_divisions` | core | reference endpoints | verify | small lookups AI needs constantly |

### 4.4 Customers / buyers / retailers
| Tool | Service | Route | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `search_customers` / `get_customer` | core / data_sync | customers endpoint | verify |

### 4.5 RFQ expansion (pricing-scoped)
| Tool | Service | Route | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `list_rfq_groups` | core | RFQ group list | verify | |
| `get_rfq_vendor_quotes` | core | RFQ vendor quotes | verify | **pricing-scoped** — costs redacted unless `pricing` scope |

### 4.6 Data sync / Coldlion
| Tool | Service | Route | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `get_data_sync_item_headers` | data_sync | item-headers endpoint (needs `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY`) | verify |

### 4.7 Aggregations for dashboards (where endpoints allow)
Counts/rollups (e.g. items by licensing status, samples at factory, prod orders by stage) to answer
operational questions without pulling full lists. Add only where a backing endpoint exists or is added.

---

## 5. How to add a tool (mechanical recipe, per this repo)

1. Add a `ToolDef` to `src/registry/tool-defs.ts` — `name`, `title`, `group`, `safety: SAFETY.readOnly`,
   `description`, `argsDescription`, `argsSchema` (zod), `exampleArgs`, `commonFailures`, `relatedTools`,
   and `invoke: async (args, { client }) => client.request(service, route, { query/body })`.
2. Add the name to `tools-config.json` → `enabled_read_tools`.
3. If a new group/keyword is needed, extend `src/registry/groups.ts` (`TOOL_GROUPS`, `KEYWORD_TO_GROUPS`).
4. Add/extend tests under `tests/unit/` (catalog/search + a client-shape test).
5. `npm run typecheck && npm test`. Keep it tier 1; never enable writes in `tools-config.json` without
   preview/confirmation/audit (per `AGENTS.md`).

---

## 6. Phased roadmap

**Phase 1 — Unblock + deploy (the critical path):**
- Add §4.1 licensing tools (+ the lean `/lic/status-export` endpoint on `designflow-tracking`).
- Add §3.1 inbound auth + §3.2 service-principal outbound auth.
- Add §3.6 `cloudbuild.yaml` + Cloud Run, point at **sandbox** BFF, register with AI clients.
- **Outcome:** AI sessions (and the poppim sync) can pull licensing status through an authenticated,
  deployed MCP. This is the milestone that makes the server real and unblocks the poppim mirror.

**Phase 2 — Breadth + protection:**
- Add §4.2–4.6 tools (prod, merch/reference, customers, RFQ, data-sync).
- Add §3.3 redaction + scopes, §3.4 audit, §3.5 rate limits/pagination.

**Phase 3 — Hardening + reach:**
- Per-consumer scoped tokens, anomaly alerting, promote to production, connect all AI clients.
- Decide §3.7 (add `run_select`/`df_ro` direct-DB layer or stay API-proxy).

**Phase 4 — Later, gated:** reversible tier-2 writes (e.g. status writeback) with preview/confirmation/audit.

---

## 7. Acceptance criteria

- `POST /mcp` rejects unauthenticated/unknown-token requests; `/health` stays open.
- Outbound calls use a read-only service principal; no human JWT in prod.
- `get_licensing_status` returns latest status + Brand Assurance # + sample # for a known item; the poppim
  sync can consume it.
- Pricing/PII fields are absent unless the consumer token carries `pricing`/`pii`.
- Every `invoke_tool` call is audited; a revoked consumer token is denied immediately.
- List tools enforce pagination + max page size; rate limit trips under abuse.
- Deployed to sandbox via Cloud Build trigger; registered and callable from an AI client.

---

## 8. Open decisions

- API-proxy only vs. **also** a `run_select` + `df_ro` direct-DB layer (§3.7).
- Exact **redact** column list (pricing/cost/margin/royalty + PII) — needs a quick policy pass.
- Consumer-token issuance/rotation and where the audit log lives.
- Read replica vs read-only role on primary (only relevant if §3.7 is adopted).

## 9. Ties to other workstreams

- **poppim licensing mirror** (`designflow-frontend/docs/licensing-poppim-phase0-bridge.md`): its permanent
  read access = the §4.1 `get_licensing_status` tool here (backed by `/lic/status-export`). This plan is the
  durable home for that "ongoing read-only access."
- **Read-only access brief** (`designflow-backend/docs/ai-readonly-access-brief.md`): the governance there
  (service principal, redaction, audit, scoped tokens, optional `df_ro`/`run_select`) is folded into §3.

content.text:
Done — committed and pushed to GitHub.

- **File:** [docs/upgrade-plan.md](designflow-mcp/docs/upgrade-plan.md) in **popcre/designflow-mcp**, plus its AGENTS.md doc-map and pending-work updated to point at it.
- **Commit:** `1f87910` on `sandbox-albert`; rode onto the existing open **[designflow-mcp#1](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-mcp/pull/1)** (`sandbox-albert` → `develop`).
- **Branch flow:** develop→sandbox-albert→local synced first (no-op — already current).

## What the plan says, in brief
It's grounded in the repo's real architecture (lazy registry, `ToolDef` + `tools-config.json`, BFF-proxy client, tier-1-only safety), and covers three things:

**1. What I/we need now (Phase 1):** the missing licensing tools — `get_licensing_status`, `get_feedback_catalog` — which become the poppim mirror's permanent read source (backed by the lean `/lic/status-export` endpoint we discussed). Plus the two things that make it real: **inbound auth on `POST /mcp`** (it's currently wide open) and **deployment** to Cloud Run sandbox via your push-to-deploy flow.

**2. Other capabilities across the whole app (Phase 2):** production tracking, merch-group/licensor-property-artist resolution, customers/buyers, RFQ groups + vendor quotes, data-sync item headers, and dashboard aggregations — each as a curated read tool, with a mechanical "how to add a tool" recipe so any future session (or your developer) can extend it consistently.

**3. The governance from the access brief, folded in (Phases 2–3):** swap the personal user JWT for a read-only **service principal**, **redact pricing/PII** behind scopes, **audit** every call, **rate-limit/paginate**, and a decision point on whether to also add the brief's `run_select`/`df_ro` direct-DB layer for true "read-everything."

It also flags the one genuinely important safety gap I found while reading the code: **the MCP HTTP endpoint has no authentication today** — so the plan makes adding that a P0 that must land with the very first deploy, before it's ever exposed.

That's the full set of docs/plans across the four repos now (frontend integration + bridge, backend access brief, this MCP upgrade plan). Whenever you want to move from planning to building, Phase 1 here is the critical path — say the word and I'll start on the licensing tools + the `/lic/status-export` endpoint.

content.text:
Thanks — got the username and password. Two things before I can use them:

**1. I still need the connection target.** A username/password alone can't reach a database — I need:
- **Host** (e.g. an IP or hostname like `db.xxx.designflow.app` or a Cloud SQL public IP)
- **Port** (Postgres default `5432`)
- **Database name** (e.g. `designflow`, or the Directus DB name)
- **Which system** these are for — the **DesignFlow** Postgres (for the `LicenseFeedBacks` status catalog) or the **poppim/Directus** Postgres (for the `stage` list and product schema)?

You can paste it as a connection string if that's easier:
`postgresql://albert_read_only:<password>@HOST:5432/DBNAME`

**2. Reachability.** The DesignFlow sandbox DB is in GCP and was private (VPC-only) — if it has no public IP, these credentials won't connect from here regardless. So tell me the host and I'll **test the connection immediately** and report whether it's reachable; if it's locked to a private network, we'll know right away and you can have it opened to an authorized IP (or we fall back to the MCP/endpoint route).

**One housekeeping note:** you pasted the password in chat, so it now lives in this conversation's history. It's read-only so the risk is low, but treat it as **rotatable** — once the MCP server is up (which is the proper home for this access), retire this account or rotate the password. I'll only ever use it for read-only queries tied to the work we've discussed.

Send me the host/port/db (and which system) and I'll connect and pull what we need.

content.text:
I checked thoroughly — fetched **all** branches and tags, and there are **no new commits anywhere** on `popcre/designflow-mcp`. The latest commit on every branch is still my plan commit `1f87910` from earlier today. So **your revised code hasn't reached GitHub** — I'm not able to read it yet.

A few likely reasons:
- The edits are **saved locally but not committed/pushed** (most common when editing with an AI tool).
- They were pushed to a **different repo or owner** (e.g. a fork, or `u2giants/...` instead of `popcre/...`).
- The push didn't complete.

To get it to me, from the folder where the revised code lives:

```powershell
git add -A
git commit -m "feat: expand MCP server with licensing and other tools"
git push origin sandbox-albert
```

Then just say "pulled" (or re-send this message) and I'll pull it and review.

**When it's up, here's exactly what I'll check** so "does it have what I need" is concrete:
1. A **`get_licensing_status`**-type tool returning, per item: latest licensing status, **Brand Assurance #**, sample #, and ideally the status history.
2. A **`get_feedback_catalog`** tool exposing the `LicenseFeedBacks` status list (for the mapping).
3. The **item number (`item_num_id`)** present in the licensing output — that's the join key the poppim sync needs to match products.
4. Nice-to-have: **inbound auth** on `POST /mcp` and a **service-principal** outbound token (the two safety items from the plan).

Push it and I'll tell you precisely whether items 1–3 (what I actually need) are covered and whether 4 is handled.

If the revised code is sitting in a folder and you're not sure whether it's even a clone of this repo, tell me the folder path and I'll check it directly from here.
